Polo Bun with Hokkaido Milk Filling

japanese: 北海道ミルクポーロパン

region_of_origin: Singapore (Hong Kong-origin polo bun style, with Hokkaido milk filling)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn entry for polo bun or Hokkaido milk bun. FOODON_03302397 (sweet bun food product) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0EJX (sweet buns and rolls) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for polo bun or Hokkaido milk bun variants.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for polo bun or Hokkaido milk bun. SNOMED CT food hierarchy does not extend to this level of specificity.

main_ingredients: cookie crust topping (flour, butter, sugar, egg)

serving.natural_language_portion: One polo bun (approximately 80 g), as sold individually at Kazo Premium bakery in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Sweet Bun / Polo Bun — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: Bread, sweet (item 18023) and Rolls, dinner, enriched (item 18350), adapted with Hokkaido milk enrichment adjustment
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for polo bun or Hokkaido milk bun exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy sweet enriched bread/roll entries (energy ~310 kcal/100g, protein ~8g, fat ~7g, carbohydrate ~53g) with upward adjustment for higher butter and milk fat content typical of Hokkaido-style enriched buns (fat raised to ~12g/100g, energy to ~340 kcal/100g). This is consistent with published nutrient profiles for Hong Kong-style polo buns in peer-reviewed food composition literature and the CSFCT (China Food Composition Table 2019) entry for 菠萝包 (polo bun, ~330–350 kcal/100g).

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of white bread and enriched sweet bun analogues — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for polo bun or Hokkaido milk bun. GI estimated at 70 based on the closest analogous food: white bread / enriched sweet rolls (GI 70–75, University of Sydney GI Database). The polo bun is a refined-flour enriched sweet bun with a cookie crust; its GI is expected to be in the high range (≥70) consistent with white bread analogues. GL per serving = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (70 × 40.8) / 100 ≈ 28.6 (serving 80g, carbohydrate 51g/100g × 0.8 = 40.8g).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~248 mg per piece / ~310 mg per 100g)
hypertension.guidance: One polo bun contributes approximately 248 mg sodium (~10% of the 2400 mg daily limit); individuals managing hypertension should account for this within their daily sodium budget and avoid pairing with other high-sodium foods in the same meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~70) and high glycemic load per serving (~28.6) from refined flour and added sugar
type2_diabetes.guidance: The polo bun has a high GI (~70) and a high GL per serving (~28.6), making it likely to cause a significant postprandial blood glucose rise; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size, pair with protein or fibre-rich foods to blunt the glycemic response, and monitor blood glucose after consumption.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~6.5g/100g, ~5.2g per piece) and trans fat (~0.2g/100g) from butter and cookie crust
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The Hokkaido milk polo bun contains moderate saturated fat and trace trans fat from butter used in both the dough and cookie crust topping; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption to occasional treats and prioritise lower-saturated-fat bakery alternatives in their regular diet.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Kazo Polo Bun (Hokkaido Milk) is a premium enriched sweet bun sold by Kazo Premium bakery in Singapore. It is a Singaporean-market adaptation of the classic Hong Kong polo bun (菠萝包, bōluo bāo), distinguished by the use of Hokkaido milk — a high-fat, creamy milk from Japan's Hokkaido prefecture — in both the soft, pillowy bread dough and the sweet cookie-crust topping that gives the bun its characteristic crackled golden surface. The name 'polo' (菠萝, meaning pineapple in Chinese) refers to the pineapple-like crackled appearance of the cookie crust, not to any pineapple flavour or filling. The Hokkaido milk variant is richer and more milky in flavour than a standard polo bun, with a tender, slightly sweet crumb and a crisp, buttery cookie crust. Each piece weighs approximately 80 g and provides around 272 kcal, 51g carbohydrate, 9.6g fat, and 6.2g protein. The bun has a high glycemic index (~70) and a high glycemic load per serving (~28.6), making it a significant source of rapidly available carbohydrate. It contains gluten, dairy, and eggs, and is not suitable for individuals with these allergies. Clinically, it is a high-GI, moderate-sodium, moderate-saturated-fat food that should be consumed in moderation by individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. It is commonly consumed as a breakfast item or afternoon snack in Singapore's café and bakery culture.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a polo bun?
What is the GI of polo bun?
Is polo bun suitable for diabetics?

confidence_reason: No exact nutrient entry for polo bun or Hokkaido milk bun exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy enriched sweet bread/roll entries with upward fat adjustment for Hokkaido-style enrichment, cross-referenced against CSFCT 2019 polo bun data. GI is an analogous estimate from white bread/enriched sweet roll data in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium due to the adapted rather than exact source match.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted estimates based on USDA SR Legacy sweet enriched bread/roll data and CSFCT 2019 polo bun reference, adjusted for Hokkaido milk enrichment. An exact laboratory analysis of the Kazo Premium polo bun would be required for high-confidence values. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy sweet bread/roll source entries used for adaptation. 3. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy sweet bread/roll source entries used for adaptation. 4. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy sweet bread/roll source entries used for adaptation. 5. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0; confirmed zero — enriched sweet buns contain negligible vitamin C, confirmed as 0 in USDA SR Legacy sweet bread entries. 6. GI is an analogous estimate (GI 70) based on white bread and enriched sweet roll data from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for polo bun is available in approved sources. 7. trans_fat_g (~0.2g/100g) is a low-level estimate consistent with butter-containing baked goods; exact value requires product-specific analysis. 8. Halal status is assumed based on typical ingredients; consumers should verify with Kazo Premium directly.
